krpano.title: Virtual Tour

hotspot.name: info1
hotspot.info_title: 1988 World Superbike Championship: The Inaugural Race
hotspot.info_text: Grasp the handlebars of the Bimota YB4 EI and drop into the very first race of the World Superbike Championship as Italian rider Davide Tardozzi. This simulation puts you on the grid for the 1988 Donington opener, challenging you to push a highly tuned production motorcycle to its absolute limit. Navigating a modernised track layout, you will engage in a relentless duel against rival riders on Ducatis. Success requires not only precision leaning and throttle control but also managing the temperamental early electronic fuel injection system that defined this pioneering era of two-wheeled racing.

hotspot.name: info2
hotspot.info_title: 1993 European Grand Prix: The Lap of the Gods
hotspot.info_text: Put yourself in the driver's seat of Ayrton Senna's McLaren MP4/8 and attempt to recreate what is widely considered the greatest opening lap in Formula 1 history. Set against the backdrop of torrential rain in 1993, this simulator throws you into fifth place on a heavily saturated Donington Park circuit. Relying on your mastery of the track evolution as the weather shifts, you must find the grip where others cannot, outbraking and outmanoeuvring technologically superior Williams-Renaults to slice through the field and claim the lead before the first lap is over.

hotspot.name: info3
hotspot.info_text: When Donington Park hosted its inaugural motorcycle race on Whit Monday in 1931, the divide between road and track was rapidly widening. While works riders like Cyril "Squib" Burton wrestled stripped-down overhead-valve racers to victory on the dirt, the thousands of fans who flocked to the circuit arrived on trusted, decade-old commuter bikes like this one. Displayed here as it would have looked parked on the grassy banks of the circuit, this Raleigh serves as a window into the spectator's experience—the reliable machine that brought the crowds to witness the birth of a racing legend.

hotspot.name: info4
hotspot.info_text: The inaugural round of the FIM Superbike World Championship on 3 April 1988 marked a historic moment for motorcycle racing, with Davide Tardozzi securing victory in the very first race of the new series. Riding the innovative Bimota YB4 EI, which featured an early electronic fuel injection system, Tardozzi engaged in a fierce battle with his Italian compatriot Marco Lucchinelli throughout the event. Despite a late technical glitch that reportedly required him to manually reset the bike's electronics, Tardozzi successfully fended off the competition to take the chequered flag. Although he dramatically crashed out of the lead on the final lap of Race 2 later that afternoon, his triumph in the opening race forever etched his name in the history books as the first-ever race winner in the World Superbike Championship, proving the formidable pace of Bimota's modified production machinery on the world stage.

hotspot.name: info5
hotspot.info_text: Marking Formula 1's return to Donington Park after 55 years, the 1993 European Grand Prix was dominated by Ayrton Senna in his Ford V8-powered McLaren MP4/8, despite facing the technologically superior active-suspension Williams-Renaults. In torrential and highly changeable conditions, Senna dropped to fifth at the start but immediately delivered the 'Lap of the Gods', overtaking four drivers to claim the lead before the end of the opening tour. While he did not win the championship that year, his masterful navigation of the wet track to lap the entire field except for second place cemented his legacy as an unparalleled wet-weather driver and produced one of motorsport's most celebrated moments.

hotspot.name: info6
hotspot.info_title: 1935 Donington Grand Prix: The Pioneer’s Challenge
hotspot.info_text: Step into the open cockpit of the Alfa Romeo Tipo-B 'P3' and experience the visceral reality of Donington's inaugural car Grand Prix. In this simulation, you take on the role of Richard 'Mad Jack' Shuttleworth during his gruelling 1935 victory. The virtual environment meticulously recreates the rudimentary 1930s circuit layout and the torrential weather conditions of the era. To succeed, you must master the heavy steering and fading drum brakes of this pre-war machine, battling against a field of Bugattis and Maseratis on a treacherous, rain-soaked track surface to claim the chequered flag.

hotspot.name: info7
hotspot.info_title: 1938 Donington Grand Prix: Mastering the Silver Arrow
hotspot.info_text: Experience the sheer power and engineering dominance of the late 1930s by taking the wheel of the mid-engined Auto Union Type D. Recreating Tazio Nuvolari’s legendary 1938 drive, this simulation challenges you to recover from an early pit-stop setback and charge through the pack. Surrounded by an immersive 360-degree recreation of the pre-war Leicestershire track, you will need to tame the immense horsepower of the German 'Silver Arrow' and carefully manage its notorious oversteer to hunt down your teammates and secure a historic, high-speed victory.

hotspot.name: info8
hotspot.info_text: Before the era of bespoke, factory-built racing machines, early British motorsport was born on the public roads. The 1922 Raleigh 350cc (2¾ hp) is a perfect representation of this grassroots era. Built with a robust side-valve engine, a Sturmey-Archer gearbox, and a utilitarian flat-tank frame, it was designed as an economical, everyday workhorse. It was precisely these types of reliable, mass-market motorcycles that early enthusiasts modified, tested in reliability trials, and eventually pushed to their limits on dirt tracks, laying the essential engineering and cultural foundations for modern circuits.

hotspot.name: info9
hotspot.info_text: The 1935 Donington Grand Prix, the circuit's inaugural event for cars, was won by privateer Richard 'Mad Jack' Shuttleworth driving an Alfa Romeo Tipo-B 'P3'. Navigating treacherous wet conditions over a gruelling 120-lap endurance test, Shuttleworth outlasted the faster but fragile Bugatti and Maserati entries, managing fading brakes to capitalise on his rivals' mechanical failures and secure the victory. Although predating the formal Formula 1 World Championship, his win cemented Donington's reputation as a world-class road racing circuit, proving its capability to host major international events and paving the way for the European factory teams that followed later in the decade.

hotspot.name: info10
hotspot.info_text: Driving the formidable, mid-engined Auto Union Type D 'Silver Arrow', Tazio Nuvolari claimed a masterful victory at the 1938 Donington Grand Prix, the circuit's final major race before the Second World War. Despite dropping down the order early on to replace a spark plug, Nuvolari charged back through the field at an astonishing pace, overtaking his teammates to win by over a minute and a half. This non-championship event demonstrated the absolute technological superiority of the state-funded German racing teams, leaving a lasting legacy of pre-war Grand Prix dominance that deeply impressed British motorsport enthusiasts.
